frameworks.name: tDCS-induced neuroplasticity
frameworks.one_line: polarity- and dose-dependent modulation of cortical excitability and activity

frameworks.name: neurovascular coupling
frameworks.one_line: CBF perfusion as proxy for stimulation-induced cortical activity

frameworks.name: finite element electric field modeling
frameworks.one_line: simulated field predicts spatial pattern of perfusion change

core_question: How do tDCS polarity and intensity over M1 jointly modulate cerebral blood flow, and do these changes track TMS-indexed cortical excitability?
key_assumptions: ASL-derived CBF indexes stimulation-driven cortical activity
TMS-evoked excitability and fMRI perfusion share a physiological basis
fixed 15-min M1 montage produces measurable aftereffects across intensities
manipulations: tDCS polarity (anodal vs cathodal vs sham)
key_findings: anodal M1 tDCS linearly increased CBF under the electrode (max 15.3% at 2.0 mA)
cathodal M1 tDCS decreased left M1 CBF from baseline across intensities
spatial distribution of perfusion change correlated with simulated electric field
TMS-indexed excitability changes correlated more with sensorimotor than hand-knob perfusion
interpretations: tDCS aftereffects on CBF are polarity- and intensity-dependent and field-localized
excitability and perfusion indices reflect related but spatially dissociable mechanisms
